User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the VirtuStyle application.

email.description: The email address of the user.

createdAt.description: The date and time the user was created.

VirtualGarment.title: VirtualGarment
VirtualGarment.description: Represents a virtual garment that can be tried on in the AR experience.

id.description: Unique identifier for the virtual garment.

name.description: The name of the virtual garment.

description.description: A description of the virtual garment.

modelUrl.description: URL to the 3D model of the garment.

imageUrl.description: URL to the image of the garment.

category.description: Category of the garment (e.g., shirt, pants, dress).

CapturedMedia.title: CapturedMedia
CapturedMedia.description: Represents a captured photo or video from the virtual try-on experience.

id.description: Unique identifier for the captured media.

mediaType.description: The type of media (e.g., photo, video).

mediaUrl.description: URL to the captured media file.

timestamp.description: The date and time the media was captured.

GestureTransfer.title: GestureTransfer
GestureTransfer.description: Represents a gesture-based transfer of media between devices.

id.description: Unique identifier for the gesture transfer.

senderId.description: Reference to User who initiated the transfer (sender). (Relationship: User 1:N GestureTransfer as Sender)

receiverId.description: Reference to User who received the transfer (receiver). (Relationship: User 1:N GestureTransfer as Receiver)

mediaId.description: Reference to CapturedMedia being transferred. (Relationship: CapturedMedia 1:N GestureTransfer)

gestureType.description: The type of gesture used to initiate the transfer.

transferStatus.description: The status of the transfer (e.g., pending, completed, failed).

timestamp.description: The date and time the transfer was initiated.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  Path-based ownership ensures only the user can read/write their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores captured media (photos, videos) for each user. Path-based ownership enables simple security rules. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: mediaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the captured media.

definition.description: Stores virtual garment information.  Accessible to all users for reading.

params.name: garmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the virtual garment.

definition.description: Stores gesture transfer requests. SenderId and ReceiverId are used in rules to control access. Does not denormalize any additional authorization data.

params.name: transferId
params.description: The unique identifier of the gesture transfer.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the VirtuStyle application's core features, including AR-based virtual try-on, media capture, and gesture-based media transfer. The structure prioritizes authorization independence, clarity, and scalability.

Users are stored in a top-level `users` collection. Captured media is stored in subcollections under each user's document (`/users/{userId}/capturedMedia/{mediaId}`). This path-based ownership ensures that only the user can access their own captured media.  The `CapturedMedia` documents include the `garmentId` to link back to the `VirtualGarment` collection.

Virtual garments are stored in a top-level `virtualGarments` collection, which is accessible to all users (for reading) to populate the AR try-on experience.  A security rule can limit write access to authorized admins.

Gesture transfers are stored in a top-level `gestureTransfers` collection. Each document contains the `senderId`, `receiverId`, and `mediaId`. This structure allows for querying transfers initiated or received by a specific user.  A membership map is not needed because security rules will ensure the sender and receiver have read access to only the transfers involving them.

**Authorization Independence**: The structure achieves authorization independence by avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules. User-owned data (captured media) is stored in subcollections under the user's document, eliminating the need to fetch user data to authorize access to the media. The `CapturedMedia` documents themselves contain the necessary information for authorization, such as the `userId`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**: The structure supports secure `list` operations. Path-based ownership for captured media enables listing only the current user's media.  The separation of virtual garments (public read) and user-specific data (private read/write) ensures that `list` operations are secure and efficient. The `gestureTransfers` collection can be secured by ensuring that only transfers where the `senderId` or `receiverId` matches the authenticated user's ID can be read.
